name: Kuala Lumpur International Airport (WMKK/KUL)
oneLiner: Departure airport; MH370 took off from runway 32R (threshold 2.74739N 101.722E) at 16:42 UTC, 7 Mar 2014.
source: OurAirports airports.csv + runways.csv (raw.githubusercontent.com/davidmegginson/ourairports-data); takeoff time FI p1 via Gilbert PDF (vridar.org)

name: Penang International Airport (WMKP/PEN)
oneLiner: Military radar tracked MH370 passing 6 NM south of Penang Island at 17:52:35 UTC during the turnback.
source: OurAirports airports.csv; 17:52:35 event FI p7 via Gilbert PDF (vridar.org)

name: Kota Bharu (Sultan Ismail Petra Airport, WMKC)
oneLiner: MH370 crossed the peninsula coast just north of Kota Bharu at ~17:37 UTC on the turnback leg; the airport's own primary radar recorded the pass at ~4 s resolution (last inbound return 17:36:54).
source: OurAirports airports.csv; coast-crossing time FI p7 via Gilbert PDF (vridar.org), mh370search.com gives 17:36:43; PSR returns from ATC-Caption-2025 decode (fetched 2026-07-16)

name: Waypoint IGARI
oneLiner: Last waypoint passed under secondary radar (17:20:31 UTC); transponder ceased and the turnback began just beyond it.
source: Wikipedia MH370 article (6°56'12"N 103°35'06"E), cross-checked with FI flight plan table N0656.2 E10335.1 via mh370wiki.net

name: Waypoint BITOD
oneLiner: Next filed waypoint after IGARI; MH370 turned back before reaching it.
source: FI filed flight plan table (N0715.4 E10407.1) via mh370wiki.net Waypoints_and_MH370

name: Waypoint GOLUD
oneLiner: One of a cluster of waypoints (with ABTOK, KADAX) near Kota Bharu that the turnback track passed over or near.
source: OpenNav (6°17'06"N 102°16'39"E); passed-near statement mh370wiki.net History of the flight

name: Waypoint PUKAR
oneLiner: Waypoint near Butterworth passed on the peninsula crossing toward Penang.
source: OpenNav (5°28'43"N 100°43'14"E); passed-near statement mh370wiki.net History of the flight

name: Waypoint VAMPI
oneLiner: Airway N571 waypoint in the Strait of Malacca; primary radar tracked MH370 passing it at 18:13 UTC.
source: OpenNav (6°10'56"N 97°35'08"E); 18:13:00 event FI p7 via Gilbert PDF (vridar.org)

name: Waypoint MEKAR
oneLiner: N571 waypoint; the last primary radar fix at 18:22:12 UTC lies 10 NM beyond it toward NILAM.
source: OpenNav (6°30'14"N 96°29'28"E); cross-validated geometrically against the published 10-NM-past-MEKAR fix 6.577655N 96.340864E (mh370search/radiantphysics)

name: Waypoint NILAM
oneLiner: Next N571 waypoint beyond MEKAR; radar contact was lost before MH370 reached it.

name: Waypoint IDKUT
oneLiner: Waypoint northwest of NILAM, relevant to post-18:22 path hypotheses (beyond recorded epochs).

name: Last secondary radar contact (17:21:13 UTC)
oneLiner: SSR position symbol dropped off the KL ATCC display 3.2 NM past IGARI (SIR Table 2.2C); end of Epoch 1. The last actual SSR return was ~30 s earlier: 17:20:42, 6.9518N 103.5978E (Kota Bharu radar head).
source: time + '3.2 nm after passing IGARI': SIR Table 2.2C p.344 / FI p2; position interpolated ~42 s past IGARI on 059° toward BITOD (official reports publish no coordinate); last actual return from ATC-Caption-2025 KB_SSR decode (fetched 2026-07-16)

name: Kate Tee yacht sighting (~19:10-19:25 UTC)
oneLiner: Sailor Kate Tee reported a low-flying aircraft 'that appeared to be descending' with 'black smoke coming from the tail' from her yacht at ~6°37.7'N 94°26.4'E; her report gives initial sighting ~19:10 UTC, closest approach ~19:20-19:25 (the UGIB 2020 model instead puts MH370's closest approach at 18:53:46). Uncorroborated eyewitness account.
source: Position and quotes: saucysailoress.wordpress.com/2017/02/02/2299/ (Gibson-assisted report, fetched 2026-07-16, HTTP 200); timing caveat per the same report vs UGIB 2020 App. E.3.5

name: Last primary radar fix (18:22:12 UTC)
oneLiner: Final military radar detection, 10 NM past MEKAR on N571 at ~29,500 ft; end of Epoch 2 (alternative Lido-slide position: 6.871123N 97.354047E).
source: mh370search.com / radiantphysics (Iannello), SIR-figure interpretation; altitude Wikipedia MH370 article

name: Pulau Perak
oneLiner: Small island in the Strait of Malacca; military radar recorded MH370 passing it at 18:02 UTC.
source: Wikipedia Pulau Perak (5°40'50"N 98°56'27"E); 18:02 time Wikipedia MH370 article

name: HA01 Cape Leeuwin hydroacoustic station (hydrophone H01W1)
oneLiner: CTBTO IMS hydrophone triad ~100 km SW of Cape Leeuwin whose recordings were analysed by Curtin University for MH370 impact signals.
source: H01W1 coordinates from Table 1 of PMC10028758 (Royal Society Open Science COVID ocean-noise study); role per mh370search.com and CTBTO

name: RAAF Learmonth Airport (YPLM)
oneLiner: Western Australian air base used as a staging point for RAAF/RNZAF search sorties over the southern Indian Ocean.
source: OurAirports airports.csv

name: Inmarsat ground earth station, Perth (PITC)
oneLiner: Ground station at the Perth International Telecommunications Centre that logged the hourly satellite handshakes defining the BTO/BFO arcs.
source: site coordinates (-31°48'09"S 115°53'07"E) from ESA Perth station page (co-located at PITC); Inmarsat GES role per Wikipedia MH370 satellite communications

name: Inmarsat-3 F1 nominal sub-satellite point
oneLiner: IOR satellite relaying MH370's handshakes; nominally geostationary at 64.5°E, drifting in an oval between 1.6°S-1.6°N and 64.45-64.58°E.
source: Wikipedia: Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 satellite communications

name: 7th arc - CSIRO/ATSB most-likely crash region
oneLiner: CSIRO drift-analysis point (35.6°S 92.8°E) on the 7th BTO arc, identified with 'unprecedented precision'; centre of the priority search region.
source: Wikipedia MH370 article, cross-checked with CSIRO news release (csiro.au, Aug 2017)

name: Broken Ridge
oneLiner: Rugged oceanic plateau in the SE Indian Ocean crossed by the 7th arc; complicated the underwater search bathymetry.
source: Wikipedia Broken Ridge (listed centre 31°S 95°E; feature ~1,200 km long)

name: Flaperon find site, Saint-André, Réunion
oneLiner: First confirmed MH370 debris - a right flaperon found on a Saint-André beach on 29 July 2015, ~4,000 km west of the search area.
source: commune coordinates Wikipedia Saint-André, Réunion (20°57'38"S 55°39'02"E; beach on NE shore, exact spot not published); find details Wikipedia MH370 / Search articles
